# Emergency Fund

An emergency fund is money set aside to cover unexpected expenses such as a job loss,
medical bill or urgent repair. A common guideline is to keep three to six months of
essential living costs in an account that is safe and easy to reach. Its purpose is to
absorb shocks without forcing someone to borrow at high interest or sell investments
at a bad time.

## Where to keep it

Because the goal is availability rather than growth, an emergency fund belongs
somewhere liquid and low-risk, like a savings account, not in volatile investments
that might be down exactly when the money is needed. Financial advisers often suggest
building a small starter fund first, then topping it up over time while also paying
down expensive debt.
